news.title: 🇬🇧 british police have arrested two teenagers over an attempted arson attack on the kenton united sy
news.summary:
🇬🇧 british police have arrested two teenagers over an attempted arson attack on the kenton united synagogue in northwest london

a 17-year-old boy and a 19-year-old man were detained after a “firebomb”, a bottle containing accelerant, was thrown through a window into a medical room. the fire caused minor smoke damage, and no injuries were reported.

counter terrorism policing is investigating whETHer the suspects are linked to a group with ties to the iranian islamic regime.

the incident is part of a series of recent arson attacks targeting jewish sites in london. 

police also reported an attempted arson at a business linked to the jewish community on friday, and earlier arrests were made over another synagogue attack last week. 

in a separate incident last month, ambulances belonging to the volunteer emergency service hatzola were set on fire in golders green.

bitmine adds 101,627 ETH in biggest weekly accumulation in 4 months  

bitmine immersion technologie
news.summary:
bitmine adds 101,627 ETH in biggest weekly accumulation in 4 months  

bitmine immersion technologies added 101,627 ETH last week, marking its fastest pace of accumulation since the week of december 15, 2025. as of april 19, 2026, the company holds a total of 4,976,485 ETH, representing approximately 4.12% of 이더리움’s total supply.  

bitmine’s total 암호화폐, cash, and investment holdings are valued at approximately $12.9 billion, including $1.12 billion in cash, 199 BTC, a $200 million stake in beast industries, and a $107 million investment in eightco holdings (orbs).

according to 코인shares, digit
news.summary:
코인shares: 암호화폐 funds see $1.4b weekly inflows, highest since january  

according to 코인shares, digital asset investment products recorded $1.4 billion in net inflows last week, marking the third consecutive week of inflows and the largest weekly inflow since january. total assets under management rose to $155 billion.  

비트코인 investment products led with $1.116 billion in inflows, bringing year-to-date inflows to $3.1 billion. 이더리움 products saw $328 million in inflows, the strongest weekly performance since january.

sulaiman ahmed (@shaykhsulaiman)

ceasefire update: iran-u.s draft proposal

✅ iran agrees
news.summary:
협상 초안이래


sulaiman ahmed (@shaykhsulaiman)

ceasefire update: iran-u.s draft proposal

✅ iran agrees to a 15-year enrichment suspension, with the exception of research reactors for medical isotopes.

✅ iran’s stockpile of 60% and 20% uranium would be partially converted to reactor fuel, and partially ‘downblended’ to a lower enrichment level. however, it won’t leave iran.

✅ full iaea supervison on iran’s nuclear sites.

✅ opening the strait of hormuz, with the possibility of collecting tolls.

✅ the u.s. will offer iran phased 제재 relief

✅ the u.s. will unfreeze all iranian assets currently frozen abroad, amounting to $20 billion or more.

✅ the u.s. will commit to a non-aggression pact with iran that is ensured through a un national security council reSOLution and a treaty ratified by congress.

✅ the u.s. will withdraw all its military forces from the persian gulf.

source: amit segal israel channel 12

news.title:
🇩🇪 germany’s economic motor shifts from global car giant to europe’s military industrial hub

german
news.summary:
🇩🇪 germany’s economic motor shifts from global car giant to europe’s military industrial hub

germany is experiencing its longest period of stagnation since world war ii. the manufacturing sector is reportedly losing around 15,000 jobs per month, while major carmakers such as volkswagen and mercedes-benz have seen profit declines of over 40%.

in response, berlin has committed more than $500 billion (€460 billion) in defense spending over the next decade, with annual military expenditure projected to reach around $190 billion by 2029.

several industrial conversions are already underway:

- rheinmetall is repurposing former auto-parts facilities in berlin and neuss for military production.

- volkswagen is reportedly discussing converting its osnabrück plant to produce defense-related components, including transport vehicles and missile systems.

- knds has acquired a former alstom rail factory in görlitz to produce components for leopard 2 tanks and puma infantry fighting vehicles.

defense companies such as henSOLdt are also recruiting engineers and technicians from automotive suppliers like continental and bosch.

the german defense industry association has nearly doubled its membership in a year, as civilian suppliers shift toward military contracts. some firms, such as schaeffler, are also expanding production into drones.

analysts estimate that raising defense spending to 3% of GDP could generate tens of billions in annual output and create up to 245,000 jobs.

📰: wsj

news.title:
🇺🇸 a 900-foot ship tried to run a blockade… bad idea.

so here’s the story, simple and straight.

a
news.summary:
🇺🇸 a 900-foot ship tried to run a blockade… bad idea.

so here’s the story, simple and straight.

a big iranian ship called touska tries to pass through a u.s. naval blockade in the gulf of oman. blockade basically means the u.s. is controlling that area and checking ships. the uss spruance warns it to stop. the crew says no.

so the navy fires, hits the engine room, stops the ship.

then u.s. marines board it and take control.

now here’s where it gets serious.

most ships you hear about in this region are just sneaking oil around 제재. that’s illegal, but pretty common.

touska is different.

this ship was already blacklisted for years by the u.s. treasury, not for oil, but for being linked to weapons-related supply chains. that means parts, chemicals, or equipment that can be used to build missiles or drones.

think of it like this. the ship isn’t the weapon. it’s the delivery truck.

then look at its route. china → malaysia → bandar abbas (iran). that path has been linked to “dual-use” cargo. that just means items that look harmless, but can also be used for military purposes.

behind all this is irisl. on paper, it’s a shipping company. but the u.s. state department says it has a history of moving sensitive military stuff, not just normal trade.

now the key line.

when donald 트럼프 says, “we’re seeing what’s on board,” he’s not just talking casually.

that means the u.s. now has:

* the cargo list (what’s inside)
* the ship logs (where it went before)
* the crew info (who’s involved)
* the route history (the whole network)

1 ship on the surface…
but what really got exposed is the entire operation behind it.

iran is calling it piracy, basically saying the u.s. had no right to do this.

but here’s the problem. to prove that, iran would have to explain what the ship was carrying and why it’s been under 제재 for so long.

and that’s where it gets uncomfortable.

meanwhile, the market reacts fast. insurance for ships in that area shoots up to around 5%. that’s huge. some companies don’t even want to insure ships there anymore.

which means every other ship captain on that route just got the message loud and clear.

so yeah, one ship got stopped.

but the bigger picture?

the u.s. just grabbed a moving piece of a supply chain… and now they can trace how the whole system works.

source: cnn, maritime executive, al jazeera, lloyd's list, @jakecan72










mario nawfal (@marionawfal)

🇺🇸🇮🇷트럼프 just floated regime change in iran as his team lands in islamabad for peace talks.

news.title: 속보: the us government has begun refunding up to $166 billion in 관세s charged under president 트럼프 afte
news.summary:
속보: the us government has begun refunding up to $166 billion in 관세s charged under president 트럼프 after the supreme court ruled the policy unlawful.

beginning today, businesses can file claims through a new customs system.

over 330,000 importers across 53 million shipments are expected to be eligible.

once approved, refunds plus interest will be paid within 60 to 90 days.

news.title:
the us wage growth gap is historically wide:

after-tax wages for higher-income households rose +5.6
news.summary:
the us wage growth gap is historically wide:

after-tax wages for higher-income households rose +5.6% yoy in march on a 3-month moving average basis, the highest in at least 3 years.

at the same time, lower-income earners saw their wages grow just+1.0% yoy, near the lowest in at least 3 years.

this marks the widest gap between the highest and lowest earners since data began in 2015.

by comparison, middle-income wages grew +2.0% yoy last month, near the lowest since mid-2024.

meanwhile, us cpi 인플레이션 jumped +3.3% yoy in march, meaning lower and middle-income households are seeing their real wages decline.

in other words, 인플레이션 is eating all of the wage gains for the bottom two-thirds of american households.

asset owners are the only winners.

news.title:
the global smartphone market is facing a historic decline:

global smartphone shipments are set to f
news.summary:
the global smartphone market is facing a historic decline:

global smartphone shipments are set to fall -13% yoy in 2026, or -160 million units, to ~1.1 billion, according to idc.

this marks a sharp drop from a +2% growth in 2025 and +6% in 2024.

the decline is being driven by an unprecedented memory chip shortage that is inflating component costs across the industry.

as a result, smartphone makers are discontinuing unprofitable entry-level models and pushing consumers toward higher-priced devices.

last year, ~170 million smartphones shipped for under $100, a segment currently uneconomical to maintain.

the shortage is expected to persist into mid-2027, and even when supply returns, memory prices are unlikely to fall back to 2025 levels.

the days of cheap smartphones are over.

쐈대


오픈컨테이너 (@opencontainerkr)

미 해군 구축함 uss spruance는 아라비아해에서 이란 국적 화물선 touska를 차단하고 약 6시간 동안 반복적으로 정지 명령을 내렸습니다. 

선박이 이에 불응하자, 미 해군은 엔진룸을 비우도록 한 뒤 5인치 함포를 발사해 추진력을 무력화했습니다.

visegrád 24 (@visegrad24)

🇯🇵 earthquakes have struck hokkaido, aomori, and iwate in japan, with a tsunami warning currently in effect. 

fishing vessels are evacuating offshore. 

a 7.4 magnitude quake occurred offshore at a depth of 10 km, with a tsunami warning of 3m for iwate and hokkaido.
